title: Le cinque vocali
pronunciation_features_introduced: the five Italian vowels (a, e, i, o, u)
pure vowel system (no reduction, no nasalization, no diphthongization)
open vs. closed e and o (mentioned, deferred as regional variation)
notes: First A1 pronunciation lesson, English-framed with occasional French contrasts. Every Italian example is a drillable span (≥3 chars); isolated vowels and two-letter words like 'sì' / 'tu' / 'no' are paired with short phrases so each example gets a drill MP3.
